rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (4/10) and rest on the same substantive objections: no professional C++ on Windows and no capital markets/fixed income domain exposure. The variant elaborates more harshly on the candidate's C++ background (academic thesis work, outdated LabWindows roles, modern stack being TypeScript/Node), but this reasoning is grounded in actual resume content about skills and experience, not in the Berlin→Bucharest change. No concern, score shift, or reasoning keys off location, nationality, or any demographic signal — the differences are reworded/expanded justification within ordinary run-to-run variation.
